name: Time-Series Subframing and Tracking Drift Analysis
description:
Astronomical time-series data can be massive. You need to crop a sequence of 20 full-frame 4K FITS images located in ~/AstroImages/time_series/raw/ to a 500x500 pixel region centered on the target star (the brightest star near X ≈ 1345, Y ≈ 2540). Save the cropped stack as an Image Sequence of FITS files in ~/AstroImages/time_series/subframes/. Measure the precise intensity-weighted centroid coordinates (X, Y) of the target star in Frame 1 and Frame 20 of the cropped stack. Calculate the tracking drift (drift_x = star_x_frame20 - star_x_frame1, drift_y = star_y_frame20 - star_y_frame1) and save a report to ~/AstroImages/time_series/tracking_report.txt with the following format:

# Tracking Drift Report
subframe_width: 500
subframe_height: 500
star_x_frame1: <value>
star_y_frame1: <value>
star_x_frame20: <value>
star_y_frame20: <value>
drift_x: <value>
drift_y: <value>
